pr.title: feat: adding some funtions (Decompiled and Matched Functions)
pr.author: damians2000

summary: Decompiled and matched small library/player-stat functions, renamed a background-flash symbol, added typed GObj/CObj setup for the background flash system, and improved Fighter x2070 bitfield names used by player attack-count telemetry. The PR also propagated the new count_thrown_items bitfield name into several fighter special-move files.
what_changed: Renamed symbol fn_8002087C to lbBgFlash_InitState in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Added baselib includes needed by lbbgflash.c for CObj loading, GObj GX linking, GObj object attachment, and GObj processes: cobj.h, gobjgxlink.h, gobjobject.h, and gobjproc.h.
Extended the local BgFlashData2 definition in lbbgflash.c with HSD_GObj* fields x40 and x44, and declared extern HSD_CObjDesc lbl_803BB028.

Implemented lbBgFlash_800209F4 in lbbgflash.c. It casts lbl_80433658 to BgFlashData2, creates two GObj instances, attaches an orthographic CObj to the first, sets up GX links, registers fn_800204C8 as a process on the second, and initializes lbl_80433658.state.active to 1 and state.mode to 0.

Implemented pl_80037BC0 in pltrick.c. It increments total attack count, increments by_attack_counts indexed by ev->x2073, and conditionally updates thrown item, aerial, special, x1A0, and x1A4 counters from Struct2070 bitfields.
Updated pltrick.h so pl_80037BC0 has a typed prototype: void pl_80037BC0(struct plAttackStats* stats, union Struct2070* ev), and added a forward declaration for struct plAttackStats.

Renamed Fighter union Struct2070 bitfields in ft/types.h from anonymous x2072_b3 through x2072_b7 names to count_x1A4, count_thrown_items, count_aerials, count_x1A0, and count_specials while preserving bit positions at fp+2072.
Updated existing fighter code in Link, Luigi, Peach, and Pikachu special-move files to use fp->x2070.count_thrown_items instead of fp->x2070.x2072_b4.
smart_moves: The PR converted previously opaque x2072 bitfield names into behavior-oriented counter flags, making the connection between fighter events and plAttackStats updates much clearer.
The pl_80037BC0 implementation reused the newly named Struct2070 flags directly, which validates the bitfield naming by showing how each flag maps to post-match attack-stat counters.
The lbbgflash.c implementation added only the baselib headers needed for the newly called APIs, helping replace placeholder declarations with typed calls.

For lbBgFlash_800209F4, storing GObj pointers into explicit BgFlashData2 fields x44 and x40 before taking local pointer-to-slot variables mirrors the observed object-slot behavior and helps express the original data ownership.

The PR propagated the count_thrown_items rename into all touched fighter call sites, avoiding a split state where the struct field had a semantic name but users still referenced the old opaque bit name.
decomp_lessons: When a mystery fighter bitfield is consumed by a stats or telemetry routine, decompilers can often derive stronger names from the destination counters rather than from the fighter-side producer alone.
A small decompiled stats function can unlock meaningful names across unrelated fighter files because many characters may set the same event flags.

For GObj setup functions, matching often requires identifying both persistent object fields in the backing data struct and temporary pointer-to-slot locals used around object creation and setup calls.

Symbol renames should be backed by function behavior. Here fn_8002087C became lbBgFlash_InitState because the PR body describes it as setting active to 0, mode to 5, and copying a duration into background-flash state.

Header prototypes should be upgraded with the implementation. pl_80037BC0 moved from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed plAttackStats and Struct2070 parameters in the same change that implemented the function.
Preserve bit offsets and comments when renaming packed bitfields. This PR kept the fp+2072 bit positions while replacing x2072_b3 through x2072_b7 with semantic names.
naming_conventions: Background flash library symbols use the lbBgFlash_ prefix, with a behavior name used when confidence allows: lbBgFlash_InitState.
Unknown or partially understood count flags in Struct2070 use count_ prefixes, with semantic categories where known: count_thrown_items, count_aerials, count_specials.
Still-uncertain counters retain offset-derived names while gaining a count_ prefix: count_x1A0 and count_x1A4.
Player attack-stat fields use explicit counter names such as total, by_attack_counts, thrown_item_count, aerials_count, specials_count, x1A0_count, and x1A4_count.
The PR keeps address-style names for functions whose behavior remains unrenamed or only partially identified, such as lbBgFlash_800209F4 and pl_80037BC0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR body records that lbBgFlash_800209F4 was only approximately matching, with a compiled stack frame of -0x18 versus an original stack frame of -0x20.

The PR body notes a callee-saved register allocation mismatch for lbBgFlash_800209F4: original r31/r30/r29 were flash/gobj1_slot/gobj2_slot, while the compiled version assigned them in a reversed order.

The stated cause of the lbBgFlash_800209F4 mismatch was variable declaration order affecting register allocation; future work on similar GObj init functions should experiment with local declaration order and pointer-to-slot locals.
Using local HSD_GObj** variables gobj1_slot and gobj2_slot appears to be a deliberate matching tactic for expressing accesses through flash->x44 and flash->x40.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human-text guidance in the slice is the PR author's explanatory body, including behavior notes and matching notes for lbBgFlash_InitState, lbBgFlash_800209F4, and pl_80037BC0.
follow_up_queries: Check whether lbBgFlash_800209F4 was later adjusted from approximately matching to fully matching, especially local declaration order and stack frame size.
Search later PRs for additional Struct2070 bitfield renames or confirmation of count_x1A0 and count_x1A4 meanings.
Find consumers of pl_80037DF4 and pl_80037ECC to see how Struct2070 events are generated and cleared.
Search for other fighter files still setting x2072_b3 through x2072_b7 or using related count flags.
Investigate whether lbBgFlash_InitState implementation exists in the final tree and whether its behavior matches the PR body notes: active = 0, mode = 5, and duration copy.
